chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI 1 C.P. No.D-1991/2013 Pakistan Telecommunication Co. V/s Federation of Pakistan & others 2 C.P. No.D-1867/2013 Engro Fertilizer Ltd. V/s Federation of Pakistan & others 3 C.P. No.D-1992/2013 Telecard Limited V/s Federation of Pakistan & others 4 C.P. V/s Federation of Pakistan & others 9 C.P. No.D-2105/2013 4B Gental International Pvt. Ltd. V/s Federation of Pakistan & others 10 C.P. No.D-2106/2013 Circle Net Communication Pak. V/s Federation of Pakistan & others 13 C.P. No.D-2125/2013 Wise Communication System Ltd V/s Federation of Pakistan & others 14 C.P. No.D-2126/2013 Linkdotnet Telecom Pvt. Ltd. V/s Federation of Pakistan & others 15 C.P. No.D-973/2018 Engro Fertilizer Ltd. V/s Federation of Pakistan & others Date Order with signature of Judge Dated: 12.09.2024 Mr. Hussain Ali Almani for petitioner in C.P. No.D-1991 and D-1867 of 2013 and C.P. No.D-973 of 2018. Mr. Abdul Ahad for petitioners in C.P. No.D-1992 to D-1996 and D- 2104 to D-2106 and D-2122, D-2124, D-2125 and D-2126 of 2013. Mr. Khaleeq Ahmed, Deputy Attorney General. Mr. Khurram Rasheed for CCP. Mr. Mehmood Abbas for respondent No.3. -.-.- The learned counsels appearing for petitioners in these petitions submit that they would be satisfied if the appeals pending before the concerned Appellate Tribunal of Competition Commission of Pakistan are decided at the earliest.

Mr. Mehmood Abbas for respondent No.3. -.-.- The learned counsels appearing for petitioners in these petitions submit that they would be satisfied if the appeals pending before the concerned Appellate Tribunal of Competition Commission of Pakistan are decided at the earliest. They further submit that till such time the appeals are decided no adverse action be taken against the petitioners. The request of M/s. Almani and Abdul Ahad, the learned counsels appearing for the petitioners, is not opposed by learned Deputy Attorney General and Mr. Khuram Rasheed, learned counsel appearing for Competition Commission of Pakistan. Order accordingly. Mr. Almani at this stage submits that he may be permitted to raise some additional grounds as raised in these petitions. The petitioners are at liberty to address and plead their case before the Tribunal and insofar as additional grounds are concerned they may seek permission from the Tribunal in this regard and the challenge to the impugned orders therein as far as additional grounds are concerned, shall be subject to the approval of the appellate forum. By consent petitions stand disposed of in the above terms along with pending applications. Chief Justice Judge
